name: FileDeleteDetected
definition: Sysmon Event ID 26 records file deletion without preserving the deleted file.
trigger_scenarios: Microsoft documents Event 26 as behavior similar to Event 23 file deletion, but without saving deleted files to the archive directory.

key_fields.explanation: The deleted file path. Focus on staged tools and scripts under C:\Users\, C:\ProgramData\, C:\Windows\Temp, or AppData.
The deleting process and stable join key to Event ID 1.
Public Sysmon field references expose hash and executable indicators that help distinguish payload deletion from routine cleanup.

false_positives: Installers, browsers, endpoint agents, and cleanup jobs delete temporary files routinely.
Unlike Event 23, Event 26 does not preserve the deleted file, so hash and process context matter more.
detection_notes: Alert when TargetFilename under C:\Users\, C:\ProgramData\, C:\Windows\Temp, or AppData is deleted by cmd.exe, powershell.exe, or an unapproved process shortly after Event 11 file creation. Microsoft documents Event 26 as file deletion without archived preservation; MITRE T1070.004 covers deletion of dropped tools and intrusion artifacts. Correlate ProcessGuid to Event 1 and use any Hashes/IsExecutable data before the artifact is lost.
kql_snippet:
Sysmon
| where EventID == 26
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Image, TargetFilename, Hashes, ProcessGuid
spl_snippet: index=sysmon EventCode=26 | table _time, host, Image, TargetFilename, Hashes, ProcessGuid
sample_log:
UtcTime: 2026-07-13 03:05:00.000
Image: C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe
TargetFilename: C:\Windows\Temp\stage.exe
Hashes: SHA256=REDACTED
IsExecutable: true
ProcessGuid: {REDACTED}

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 26
queries.query:
Event
| where EventLog == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" and EventID == 26
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Sysmon Operational channel is collected into Event.
Map raw XML fields to the event-specific fields described on this page.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 26
queries.query:
index=sysmon source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=26
| table _time, host, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and parsed field names vary by deployment.
Apply the documented field conditions after validating local field extraction.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 26
assumptions: Confirm Provider is Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon because event IDs are not globally unique.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed names vary by collection path.

queries.title: Sysmon Event ID 26
queries.query:
title: Sysmon Event ID 26
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: sysmon
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 26
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the event-specific conditions and correlations documented on this page.

value_references.meaning: A file deletion was detected without archiving the deleted file.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret EventID with the other documented fields and correlated events; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: The deleted file path used for triage and correlation.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret TargetFilename with the other documented fields and correlated events; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Sysmon Event ID 26 mean?
faqs.answer: A file was deleted and logged without preserving an archive copy.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 26 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: No. Applications and users delete files continuously.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 26 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Review TargetFilename, Image, hashes when available, and ProcessGuid-linked creation and execution activity.
